description_long: This is a small, hand-tied bouquet of dried flowers featuring a mix of orange-red and beige seed pods, surrounded by faded green and brown foliage. The bouquet is bound together with a green string near the base, holding the slender, woody stems tightly. The flower heads are globular and spiky, with some resembling dried thistle or globe amaranth, and others appearing as papery seed capsules. The leaves are mostly shriveled and curled, with a few retaining a lanceolate or ovate shape. The overall appearance is rustic and natural, with a muted color palette typical of dried floral arrangements. This bouquet would commonly be found as a decorative element in urban homes, cafes, or boutique shops.
description: A hand-tied bouquet of dried orange-red and beige flowers with woody stems and faded green foliage, bound by a green string. The arrangement features spiky, globular flower heads and curled leaves, giving it a rustic, natural look.
description_view_0: Front view shows the bouquet's dense cluster of orange-red and beige flower heads, with the green string binding the stems clearly visible.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the rounded seed pods and the arrangement of the stems, with more beige pods visible and the bouquet appearing slightly narrower.
description_view_2: The back view reveals more of the faded green and brown foliage, with fewer flower heads visible and the stems more prominent.
description_view_3: This side view emphasizes the spread of the bouquet, with the spiky flower heads and curled leaves extending outward, and the green string still visible.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial and somewhat whorled configuration, with some overlapping and layering around the central seed pod or flower head. The arrangement lacks strict symmetry due to the dried and curled state.
